title: aquilaria baillonii
intro: Aquilaria baillonii, also known as the 'African eaglewood', is a rare and endangered tree species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae. The medicinal properties of Aquilaria baillonii are attributed to its resin, which contains bioactive compounds with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Aquilaria baillonii is typically used to prepare herbal remedies such as essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
